# Modern rgb()/hsl() syntax

<FeatureLinks mdn="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/search?q=%E7%8E%B0%E4%BB%A3%20rgb%2Fhsl%20%E8%AF%AD%E6%B3%95%20CSS" caniuse="https://caniuse.com/?search=%E7%8E%B0%E4%BB%A3%20rgb%2Fhsl%20%E8%AF%AD%E6%B3%95" status="Color 4" label="Reference links" />

## Plain-language explanation

Use space-separated channels and slash alpha across modern color functions.

## Representative syntax

```css
rgb(255 0 0 / 50%), hsl(120 80% 40% / .8)
```

## Demo

<CssDemo title="Modern rgb()/hsl() syntax" css-b64="" code-b64="" html-b64="" caption="The important part is the relationship between colors: one semantic color generates a maintainable set of states." lang="en-US" badge="Practical demo" />

## When to use it

<div class="usage-grid">
<section>
  <h3>Best fit</h3>
  <p>Use Modern rgb()/hsl() syntax in theme and state systems when you need to build color variations from semantic inputs instead of hand-tuning every state. It usually gives the stylesheet a clearer job than extra classes, wrappers, or script.</p>
</section>
<section>
  <h3>Example</h3>
  <p>A warning banner can derive its background, border, and text color from one token and still adapt in dark mode.</p>
</section>
<section>
  <h3>Watch out</h3>
  <p>Perceptual spaces help with ramps, but they do not replace contrast checks against real text sizes and backgrounds.</p>
</section>
</div>

- Representative syntax: `rgb(255 0 0 / 50%), hsl(120 80% 40% / .8)`
